title: hieracium linguans
side_effects: Hieracium linguans can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, diarrhea, and stomach cramps, as well as allergic reactions, which may manifest as skin rashes, itching, and difficulty breathing. Prolonged use or excessive dosage can also lead to more severe complications, including liver damage and kidney impairment. Additionally, Hieracium linguans can interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, potentially exacerbating underlying health conditions. In extreme cases, improper use of Hieracium linguans can even cause seizures, coma, or even death.
